title: Request Context, Deadlines, Cancellation, and Cleanup · Runtime Contract: Deadline Budget Allocation
summary: A request starts database and outbound work with background contexts, ignores disconnects, exhausts its entire deadline before cleanup, and leaks goroutines after middleware returns. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo deadline budget allocation increment in deadline budget and cancellation propagation trace. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Allocate caller deadline across validation, database, outbound dependency, response, and cleanup reserves instead of stacking independent full timeouts.
Trace when an incoming request context is canceled and pass it through every request-scoped operation without storing it in long-lived structs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Deadline Budget Allocation · Theory Deadline Budget Allocation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 4858 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive a dependency budget while reserving time for cancellation cleanup; then saturate one admission budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Deadline Budget Allocation contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Go server Predict Deadline Budget Allocation · Theory Deadline Budget Allocation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for allocate caller deadline across validation, database, outbound dependency, response, and cleanup reserves instead of stacking independent full timeouts. For Go server Predict Deadline Budget Allocation · Theory Deadline Budget Allocation, keep Deadline Budget Allocation connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
